# Requirements Checklist: US4 Plugin Lifecycle

**Purpose**: Validate US4 requirements quality (plugin install/uninstall/enable/disable/inspect/update scope) before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-04-29
**Feature**: [spec.md](specs/001-whistle-cli-ai/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are requirements defined for plugin identity input formats (name, scoped name, version spec) and validation rules? [Gap, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for plugin installation sources (npm registry only vs local tarball/git) and which are explicitly out of scope for v1? [Gap, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for the expected lifecycle states (installed/enabled/disabled/unknown) and the source of truth for each? [Completeness, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for uninstall behavior when a plugin is not installed (idempotency vs error)? [Coverage, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements defined for update semantics (update to latest vs specific version, and whether it implies enable/disable changes)? [Gap, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements defined for what metadata must be returned by `plugins inspect` (version, description, homepage, capabilities, config hints)? [Completeness, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK007 Are requirements defined for multi-instance behavior (plugin operations apply to current instance vs global environment)? [Gap, Spec FR-001 + US4]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK008 Is the term "manage plugins" clarified into a stable command surface mapping (list/install/uninstall/enable/disable/inspect) without ambiguity? [Clarity, Contracts resource-commands.md]
- [ ] CHK009 Are "reports any registry, version, or compatibility issue" requirements defined with specific error categories and machine-readable codes? [Clarity, Spec US4 + Contracts output-contract.md Error Contract]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the boundary for "update" clearly defined vs "install" (e.g., upgrade existing vs fresh install) and is the default behavior deterministic? [Ambiguity, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "metadata inspection" defined as which fields are required vs optional, and which are best-effort? [Clarity, Spec US4]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do plugin lifecycle requirements align with FR-019 (no unified plugin custom-action invocation in v1) without contradicting shortcut expectations? [Consistency, Spec FR-019]
- [ ] CHK013 Are plugin commands consistently treated as resource commands with the output envelope contract (`status/resource/action/meta`)? [Consistency, Contracts output-contract.md]
- [ ] CHK014 Is rollback scope consistent with FR-015: which plugin mutations are rollbackable and what rollback means (uninstall vs revert version vs re-enable)? [Consistency, Spec FR-015]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Are US4 acceptance scenarios mapped to concrete commands and flags (e.g., `plugins install`, `plugins enable`, `plugins inspect`)? [Gap, Spec US4 + Contracts resource-commands.md]
- [ ] CHK016 Can "preserving the user’s existing Whistle environment" be verified objectively (what counts as environment changes; rules/values side-effects)? [Measurability, Spec US4]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are primary flows specified for: install -> enable -> inspect -> disable -> uninstall? [Coverage, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK018 Are alternate flows specified for: install already installed, enable already enabled, disable already disabled? [Coverage, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK019 Are exception flows specified for: network/registry failure, incompatible Node/Whistle version, permission denied, invalid plugin name? [Coverage, Spec US4 + Spec FR-013]
- [ ] CHK020 Are recovery flows specified for partial install/update failures (e.g., rollback strategy, next actions, manual cleanup)? [Gap, Spec FR-013 + FR-015]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for plugin name resolution ambiguity (multiple matches, plugin vs @scope/plugin)? [Gap, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for dependency conflicts (peer deps) and how they are surfaced to the user/AI? [Gap, Spec US4]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements defined for offline mode / air-gapped environments and what behavior is expected? [Gap, Spec FR-018]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK024 Are performance expectations defined for plugin operations (timeouts, long-running installs) and their output behavior (streaming vs polling)? [Gap, Plan Performance Goals]
- [ ] CHK025 Are security requirements specified for plugin supply chain risks (e.g., warnings for unknown publishers, checksum, audit guidance) or explicitly declared out of scope? [Gap, Spec FR-020]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Are dependencies between raw backend (`w2`) and plugin lifecycle explicitly stated (must `w2` be installed, or can it fall back)? [Gap, Tasks T057 + Spec FR-018]
- [ ] CHK027 Are assumptions documented about where plugins are installed (global vs instance-local) and how that maps to multi-instance support? [Gap, Spec FR-001 + US4]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK028 Is it explicit whether `plugins update` exists as a first-class command in v1 (it appears in US4 test narrative but not in resource contract list)? [Ambiguity, Spec US4 + Contracts resource-commands.md]
- [ ] CHK029 Is it explicit how plugin lifecycle commands interact with rollback logging and `--rollback <action-id>` shared flag semantics? [Ambiguity, Contracts resource-commands.md + Spec FR-015]
- [ ] CHK030 Are error codes for plugin failures fully enumerated beyond `PLUGIN_NOT_INSTALLED` / `PLUGIN_CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE` (e.g., registry unreachable, invalid package, permission)? [Gap, Contracts output-contract.md Error Codes]

## Notes

- 勾选完成用 `[x]`，建议在条目后追加简短备注（例如：具体缺口在哪一段、建议补充的字段/例子）。
